seoTitle: How to Edit Labels in Microsoft Loop (2026)
metaDescription: Rename, remove, or manage custom label options inside Microsoft Loop tables, including a keyboard workaround for unresponsive label menus.
intro: Stale category names in a Loop table can quickly become confusing, especially if the label editing menu doesn't respond as expected. This guide covers the quick way to rename a label directly, the more thorough label group editor, and a keyboard workaround for common cursor-focus issues.

steps.heading: Open the relevant column
steps.body: Open your Loop page canvas and navigate to the specific table column with the labels you need to update.

steps.heading: Try a quick rename
steps.body: Click directly inside an existing label cell, type your new name, and press Enter to sync it across all shared channels.

steps.heading: Or edit the full label group
steps.body: Click the column header, select 'Change column type,' and choose Label to open the full management drawer.

steps.heading: Use the keyboard workaround if needed
steps.body: If the cursor fails to select option fields properly, use your keyboard arrow keys to navigate and type new label values.

steps.heading: Save or delete labels
steps.body: Click Save to apply changes, or click the X icon on an individual label to permanently delete it from the matrix.

faq.question: Why won't the label editor respond to my mouse clicks?
faq.answer: This is a known UI quirk with custom label groups — switching to keyboard arrow key navigation usually resolves it.

faq.question: Do renamed labels update instantly for everyone?
faq.answer: Yes, Loop's data management layer syncs label updates live across Teams, Outlook, and other connected collaborative instances.

faq.question: What happens if I delete a label that's still in use on some rows?
faq.answer: Deleting a label removes it from the available options, so rows using it may need to be reassigned to a remaining label afterward.

faq.question: What should I do if the table's column options appear locked?
faq.answer: If editing options seem unresponsive during a complex session, try creating a brand-new page and rebuilding the table as a workaround.
